bottom_line: The production fix was correct (3 LOC), but S04 required 4 fix-cycles due to ambiguous test-file guidance, and three QV gates (S07, S10, S11) each failed once on their first run — all on pre-existing test code outside I-00111's scope. Tightening the design-doc's test import rules and making QV gates skip pre-existing test failures would have cut this item's iteration count substantially.
coverage_notes: Full logs read for S01–S09 (all small). S10–S13 (>400 KB each) sampled via tail+grep. S05_run1: 0 bytes (anomaly). S14_run1: empty (current step). DB signal: full (item-status JSON retrieved).

findings.title: S04 CodeReview fix-cycle oscillation on TestClient import location
findings.recommendation: Tighten the 'Test-file location' section of the design-doc template with concrete before/after examples for the TestClient import pattern. Add an explicit rule: 'Runtime imports (TestClient) stay at module top; factory functions (create_app) are lazily imported inside fixtures or test functions.'
evidence: ai-dev/logs/I-00111_S04_fix1.log:1 — 'moved TestClient inside if TYPE_CHECKING: block'
ai-dev/logs/I-00111_S04_fix3.log:1 — 'Moved TestClient from the TYPE_CHECKING guard to a runtime import (top-level, before if TYPE_CHECKING:)' ← contradicts fix2
ai-dev/logs/I-00111_S04_fix4.log:1 — 'create_app removed from module top, imported lazily inside the test function' ← 3rd different fix
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Add concrete before/after examples to the Test-file location section of the design-doc template to resolve S04 fix-cycle oscillation in I-00111 (see ai-dev/work/I-00111/reports/I-00111_self_assess_report.md). Target file: templates/design/Incident_Design_Template.md. Effort: S.

findings.title: QV-gate assertion fix introduced a type error that surfaced in the next gate
findings.recommendation: The assertion scanner (scripts/check_assertions.py) should flag comparisons between a variable typed as list[...] and a frozenset literal as a potential type mismatch. The 'fix' prompt for tautological assertions should include a type-check step before the agent commits the change.
evidence: ai-dev/logs/I-00111_S10_run1.log:3571 — 'AssertionError: ... got ["tests/**", "**/*.md"]' (type mismatch in next gate)
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr Add type-check step to assertion-scanner fix recommendations to prevent list vs frozenset mismatches (see ai-dev/work/I-00111/reports/I-00111_self_assess_report.md). Target file: scripts/check_assertions.py. Effort: M.

findings.title: Three independent QV-gate first-run failures on pre-existing test code
findings.recommendation: Add a pre-flight check in QV-gate agents that skips failures in files not modified by the current item. If only pre-existing tests fail, pass with a warning rather than failing the gate. This requires checking git diff --name-only against the pytest failure report.
evidence: ai-dev/logs/I-00111_S07_run1.log:1 — 'tautology ... make: *** Error 1' (I-00107 tests flagged, outside I-00111 scope)
ai-dev/logs/I-00111_S10_run1.log:3868 — '1 failed ... make: *** Error 1' (type mismatch from S07 fix, same file)
ai-dev/logs/I-00111_S11_run1.log:1 — '2 failed ... make: *** Error 1' (overflow tests, pre-existing rot)
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-cr QV gates should skip pre-existing test failures and pass with warning when failures are in files outside the item's diff (see ai-dev/work/I-00111/reports/I-00111_self_assess_report.md). Target file: skills/iw-qv-gate/SKILL.md. Effort: M.

findings.title: S05 CodeReview run1 log is empty (0 bytes)
findings.recommendation: Add a size-gate in the executor or log aggregator that alerts when a run log is 0 bytes. Investigate whether the first run was silently skipped or its output was lost.
findings.paste_prompt: /iw-new-incident Add size-gate for 0-byte run logs in executor log capture (see ai-dev/work/I-00111/reports/I-00111_self_assess_report.md). Target file: executor/ (bash scripts). Effort: S.
